key_insight: Cayley graphs encode enough geometric and spectral structure to learn and compare finite-group properties directly from graph observables.
engineering_relevance: This work provides a large benchmark for graph-based property prediction in algebraic data, useful for designing interpretable graph ML pipelines and feature sets.
method: Built a dataset of 131,406 Cayley graphs for all groups of order at most 767 except 512, with algebraic labels and graph statistics.
Computed cycle, distance, spectral, and disorder-based features to probe which observables correlate with group properties.
Compared classical models, an MLP, and GNNs (notably GIN and sometimes GCN) for predicting algebraic labels from graphs.
evidence_signal: The evaluation spans 131,406 labeled graphs and shows engineered graph statistics are highly informative, while GIN and some fixed-order GCN settings recover substantial structural signal from the raw graphs.
watch_item: Results may depend on group order and feature availability, so fixed-order model performance and generalization beyond the sampled orders should be treated cautiously.
paper_title: Learning the Graphical Nature of Symmetries
